DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID KLEIN , AN INDIVIDUAL DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with and without leave to amend.
The First Cause of Action for Breach of Contract is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND because it is barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP section 337(a).) Plaintiff advances three overarching breaches of the Brokerage Agreement: (1) breach of Klein's fiduciary duties, including duty of loyalty and care; (2) failing to obtain Plaintiff's informed consent that Klein would be acting as a dual agent; and (3) failing to provide Plaintiff with an unredacted master lease and disclosing the amount the master tenant was paying in rent. (TAC 38-45.) Any breach of Klein's fiduciary duties, including failure to obtain consent for dual agency, are duplicative allegations supporting the cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. To the extent one could read the allegations with respect to the undisclosed rent amount as a breach of an actual term in the brokerage agreement, Plaintiff was on inquiry notice and possessed a factual basis for suspicion. (E-Fab, Inc. v. Accountants, Inc. Services (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1308, 1326.) Once a plaintiff becomes aware of facts which would make a reasonably prudent person suspicious, the duty to investigate arises and the plaintiff may then be charged with knowledge of the facts which would have been discovered by such an investigation. (Bedolla v. Logan & Frazer (1975) 52 Cal.App.3d 118, 131.) Plaintiff retained Klein in or about 2013 to act as Plaintiff's real estate broker to find a space in which to operate plaintiffs' art gallery business. (TAC, 11.) Klein located a "sublease space" that was leased by defendant Gross Art Fine Art Inc. (Master Tenant). (TAC 13, 14.) Plaintiff entered into a written sublease on March 1, 2013. Plaintiff alleges she was provided a copy of the Master Lease that included a redaction of the amount of rent paid by defendant Gross Art Fine Art Inc. (TAC, 28.) Plaintiff admittedly saw the redaction, which prompted her to ask Klein, generally, "what had been redacted in the Master Lease," to which Klein allegedly responded that it was "nothing important." However, one can clearly see on page 1 of the Master Lease (attached as exhibit 2), that the amount under heading (h), labeled "Annual Base Rental Rate" was "Redacted." Plaintiff does not allege she asked Klein why the annual rental amount was redacted, nor does she allege that she asked Klein for a copy of an unredacted copy of the Master Lease with the annual rent amount. A plaintiff relying on the discovery rule must plead... "the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence." (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808.) Plaintiff cannot plead an inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence because no efforts to learn about the amount of rent Gross Fine Art Inc. paid were made despite being put on inquiry notice. Plaintiff's reliance on Klein's response does not save Plaintiff here. "[E]ven assuming for the sake of argument that each of the respondents had a fiduciary duty to plaintiffs, this does not mean that plaintiffs had no duty of inquiry if they were put on notice of a breach of such duty. (WA Southwest 2, LLC v. First American Title Ins. Co. (2015) 240 Cal.App.4th 148, 157.) "Reasonable diligence in such circumstances does not consist of ignoring [redacted versions of documents] received prior to [entering into a lease]." (Id. at 157.) The Third Cause of Action for Breach of Contract Implied in Fact; Fourth Cause of Action for Intentional or Negligent or Reckless Breach of the Covenant of Good faith and fair dealing; and the Sixth Cause of Action for Denial of Contract, based on the same allegations as the first cause of action, are equally barred by the statute of limitations and SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT BRIAN GROSS , AN INDIVIDUAL, BRIAN GROSS FINE ART, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND in its entirety. Plaintiff was put on notice that the Court would grant final leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of each cause of action by each Plaintiff against each of the moving defendants. Plaintiff failed to do so.
The Second Cause of Action for Breach of Contract is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND because it is barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP section 337(a).) Plaintiff was in inquiry notice and there are no allegations that the Gross Defendants did anything to prevent Plaintiff from learning the amount of rent under the Master Lease.
The Fifth Cause of Action for Intentional or Reckless or Negligent Breach of The Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. There is a "general rule precluding tort recovery for noninsurance contract breach, at least in the absence of violation of 'an independent duty arising from principles of tort law' [citation] other than the bad faith denial of the existence of, or liability under. the breached contract." (Freeman & Mills. Inc. v. Belcher Oil Co.(1995) Cal.4th 85, 102.) Here, the contract is not an insurance contract, and Plaintiff has not pleaded a violation of any duty arising out of tort law. Rather, Plaintiff has merely pleaded that the Gross Defendants acted in bad faith in breaching the Sublease. (See, ibid.)
The Ninth Cause of Action for Fraud and Deceit is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The claim is barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP section 338(a).) Plaintiff fails to allege any affirmative misconduct by the Gross Defendants after failing to disclose the rent, that prevented Plaintiff from discovering the rent under the Master Lease. In addition, the Third Amended Complaint like the previous complaints does not plead sufficient facts to meet the heightened pleading standard required for a claim of fraud. Plaintiff does not set forth specific facts as to whom and when the misrepresentation regarding the rent amount was made. (TAC, 100.)
The Twelfth Cause of Action for Intentional or Reckless or Negligent Interference with Protected Property Interests, Economic Relationships, Partnerships Relations and Prospective Economic Advantage is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff failed to allege the existence of any existing economic relationship between themselves and an identifiable third party; failed to allege the Gross Defendants' knowledge of that/those relationship; failed to allege any acts by the Gross Defendants designed to disrupt such a relationship; failed to allege actual disruption of any relationship; and failed to allege any economic harm caused by the Gross Defendants' disruptive acts.
The Thirteenth Cause of Action for Conversion is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The Gross Defendants did not collect any funds beyond what was expressly stated in the payment terms outlined in the Sublease agreement. Moreover, "[t]he law is well settled that there can be no conversion where an owner either expressly or impliedly assents to or ratifies the taking, use or disposition of his property." (Farrington v. A. Teichert & Son, Inc. (1943) 59 Cal.App.2d 468, 474.)
The Fourteenth Cause of Action for Constructive Trust is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.
The Fifteenth Cause of Action for Unjust Enrichment is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. "[T]here is no cause of action in California for unjust enrichment." (Melchior v. New Line Productions, Inc. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 779, 793, 131 Cal.Rptr.2d 347.)
